mechanism_narrative: Higher FEDFUNDS levels raise the discount rate applied to near-term equity cash flows and tighten financing conditions, so an always-on signal that increases with FEDFUNDS predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS observations do not produce lower subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower FEDFUNDS observations in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is greater than -0.03 in absolute failure direction over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when retail sales growth is positive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth observations have equal or lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than non-positive RSAFS growth observations, or if the positive-growth hit rate is below 50% after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a labor-market stress threshold signals deteriorating income growth and recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 5.0% after being at or below 5.0% in the prior 3 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE threshold crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS is already elevated, inflation pressure forces restrictive policy and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-positive and FEDFUNDS-above-threshold condition does not underperform periods with only one condition active, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing INDPRO indicates stronger real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, so positive industrial production momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month momentum does not deliver higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO momentum, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A downward threshold break in HOUST signals weakening housing investment and future demand for durable goods, leading investors to reduce cyclical earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1800 after being above 1800 in the prior monthly observation
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside threshold breaks are not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-break periods, or if the triggered downside signal has a positive hit rate above 60%.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with a falling UNRATE identifies broad labor income strength without slack deterioration, supporting consumption and earnings revisions that produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change is positive and UNRATE 1-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-falling condition fails to outperform cases where only PAYEMS rises, or if average 5-day forward returns after triggers are not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO together with rising CPIAUCSL identifies stagflationary pressure: real activity weakens while input costs rise, causing margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is negative and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint falling-INDPRO and rising-CPIAUCSL condition does not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional periods, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.
